item.title: Hat-trick hero Dembele displays Ballon d'Or brilliance
item.description: Ousmane Dembele produced his best ever World Cup performance with a stunning first-half hat-trick in France's 4-1 win over Norway on Friday, and then insisted it was his least impressive performance of this year's tournament so far....

item.title: Uruguay crash out as Spain avoid Argentina clash
item.description: Uruguay bowed out of the World Cup as goalkeeper Fernando Muslera's howler gifted Spain a 1-0 win in Guadalajara to secure top spot in Group H. ...

item.title: Partho calls for zakat database, Islamic banking push, Bhola development plan
item.description: Bangladesh Jatiya Party lawmaker Andaleeve Rahman Partho on Saturday called for the creation of a national database of zakat recipients, greater promotion of Islamic banking and a dedicated development programme for Bhola, saying the measures could strengthen both the country’s economy and social welfare......

item.title: Man stabbed to death during Tazia procession, 4 arrested
item.description: According to the police, Zakir was participating in a Tazia procession brought out from Bibika Rowza Imambara under Sutrapur police station on Friday afternoon......

item.title: Cape Verde extend World Cup fairytale to set up Argentina meeting
item.description: World Cup debutants Cape Verde will face reigning champions Argentina in the knockout rounds after drawing 0-0 with Saudi Arabia on Friday to extend their fairytale journey....

item.title: US, Iran trade strikes putting new strain on ME ceasefire
item.description: The United States and Iran traded military strikes after Washington on Friday accused Tehran of attacking a cargo ship, jeopardising a fragile ceasefire as diplomats struggle to contain the Middle East war....

item.title: Police seek to show ailing journalist Azahar arrested in fresh terrorism case
item.description: The police have sought permission to show arrested critically ill senior journalist Azahar Ali Sarker in a fresh case under the Anti-Terrorism Act, 2009, despite his securing interim bail from the High Court in an earlier case, a decision that the Appellate Division subsequently upheld....

item.title: Police seek to show arrest critically ill journalist Azahar in fresh terrorism case
item.description: Police have sought permission to show arrested critically ill senior journalist Azahar Ali Sarker in a fresh case under the Anti-Terrorism Act, 2009, despite his securing interim bail from the High Court in an earlier case and the Appellate Division subsequently upholding that bail......

item.title: Messi to start dead-rubber against Jordan on bench
item.description: Lionel Messi will start Argentina's final group game against Jordan on Saturday on the bench with the reigning champions already assured of top spot, coach Lionel Scaloni said....

item.title: Jamaat MP accuses home minister of failing to maintain law and order
item.description: Jamaat-e-Islami lawmaker from Sirajganj-4 Rafiqul Islam Khan has said that the home minister was competent in all areas except maintaining the law-and-order situation of the country......

item.title: Opportunities for newcomers as Bangladesh resume Test journey
item.description: Opportunities for the newcomers loom as Bangladesh are set to resume their busy schedule in Test cricket, with the first match against Zimbabwe starting Sunday at the Harare Sports Club in Harare. ...

item.title: Egypt coach optimistic about Salah
item.description:
Egypt coach Hossam Hassan said he was optimistic about the health of Mohamed Salah despite the star striker's second-half exit during his country's 1-1 World Cup draw with Iran.
...

item.title: Pino, Williams injuries mar Spain's progress
item.description:
Spain's progress to the last 32 of the World Cup came at a cost as Yeremy Pino's tournament appears over, while Nico Williams suffered another muscle injury in Friday's 1-0 win over Uruguay.
...

item.title: Ex-adviser Mahfuj warns of being ‘scapegoats’, calls for unity
item.description: Former interim government adviser and a key figure in the 2024 July mass uprising, Mahfuj Alam on Saturday warned that he could become the ‘first scapegoat’ but not the last, saying others would also be targeted one by one, in a Facebook post that appeared to call for unity amid growingpoliticaltensions......Politics

item.title: Mahfuj warns of ‘scapegoats’, calls for unity in Facebook post
item.description: Former interim government adviser and a key figure in the 2024 July mass uprising, Mahfuj Alam on Saturday warned that he could become the ‘first scapegoat’ but not the last, saying others would also be targeted one by one, in a Facebook post that appeared to call for unity amid growingpoliticaltensions......Politics

item.title: Bielsa accepts blame for World Cup exit
item.description:
Marcelo Bielsa said Uruguay deserved more from all three of their World Cup games, but accepted the critics that will come his way after the two-time winners crashed out in the group stage.
...

item.title: Iran captain suggests side not welcome in US
item.description: Iran captain Mehdi Taremi questioned whether his side were welcome at the World Cup, strongly criticising the conditions they are playing under in the United States after they drew 1-1 with Egypt on Friday to possibly secure a knockout spot....

item.title: BNP lawmaker Farroque questions Jamaat about its position in 1971
item.description: Ruling party lawmaker Zainul Abdin Farroque on Saturday criticised Jamaat-e-Islami’s role during the 1971 War of Independence, questioning the party’s position at the time and its involvement in wartime atrocities......

item.title: Cape Verde exploits 'show our country to the world'
item.description: Cape Verde's coach said their World Cup exploits had ‘shown our country to the world’ after setting up a last-32 meeting with Lionel Messi's reigning champions Argentina on Friday....

item.title: Seven more children die from measles, symptoms in 24 hours across Bangladesh
item.description: Seven more children died from measles and measles symptoms in 24 hours until 8:00am on Saturday, bringing Bangladesh’s combined tally of confirmed and suspected measles-related deaths to 709......

item.title: Injured Australian pair Leckie, Italiano out of World Cup
item.description: Injured Australian pair Mathew Leckie and Jacob Italiano were ruled out of the World Cup Friday ahead of their last-32 clash with Egypt, leaving the Socceroos to complete the tournament with 24 players....

item.title: Belgium into World Cup last 32 with New Zealand rout
item.description: Belgium defeated New Zealand 5-1 to book their place in the knockout rounds of the World Cup on Friday, securing their first win of the tournament to finish on top of Group G in Vancouver....

item.title: Iran have certain last-32 place snatched away
item.description:
Iran were deprived of a certain place in the World Cup knockout phase when a goal in stoppage time that would have given them a 2-1 win against Egypt was disallowed.
...

item.title: Opposition MP Shahjahan says budget favours businessmen’s interests only
item.description: Opposition lawmaker Shahjahan Chowdhury on Saturday said that the finance minister had presented a business-friendly budget rather than one reflecting the interests of Bangladesh’s 18 crore people, alleging that this was influenced by the minister’s background in a business family......

item.title: Changing names, uniforms does not change mentality: BNP MP
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party lawmaker Gias Uddin Quader Chowdhury told the Jatiya Sangsad on Saturday that changing uniforms or names does not bring any change in mentality......
